claims.claim_text: Most types do not need custom =destroy. The compiler auto-manages destruction for primitives, string, seq[T], ref T, array, tuples, closures, and objects whose fields are all auto-managed.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. All listed types are destroyed automatically under ORC without custom hooks.

claims.claim_text: The compiler lifts hooks through arbitrary nesting: if a field's type already has custom hooks, the enclosing type gets correct compiler-generated hooks for free.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Enclosing type gets correct compiler-generated hooks that call field hooks.

claims.claim_text: Custom hooks are needed when the type holds raw pointers (ptr T) to manually allocated memory, OS file descriptors, socket handles, distinct types used as handles, or any non-managed resource.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. ptr T fields are NOT auto-managed; custom =destroy required.

claims.claim_text: Export custom ownership hooks in reusable modules. Missing export may still work in a single module but can break or complicate cross-module use.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED as a practical rule. Exported hooks work, while single-module behavior alone is not enough evidence that an unexported hook is safe for reuse.

claims.claim_text: Use =trace only for ORC-aware manually allocated containers that can participate in cycles.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. =trace compiles and runs under ORC.

claims.claim_text: =destroy should release only resources the object still owns. Check the moved-from sentinel first.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Sentinel check prevented double-free. Destroy after wasMoved was a no-op.

claims.claim_text: =wasMoved should reset the fields that make =destroy a no-op.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. After wasMoved, destroy was a no-op.

claims.claim_text: Compiler can synthesize =sink from =destroy plus copyMem. No custom =sink needed by default.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. expandArc confirms synthesized sink works correctly.

claims.claim_text: Do not add self-assignment checks to =sink. Simple self-assignments are transformed into a no-op by the compiler.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Custom =sink NOT called for x=x.

claims.claim_text: =copy needs self-assignment protection. Without it, x=x destroys the source before copying.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Self-copy with pointer comparison did not crash.

claims.claim_text: For move-only types, prefer {.error.} on =copy. Custom error messages are NOT emitted by the compiler.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Only bare {.error.} prevents the copy.

claims.claim_text: =dup with {.nodestroy.} is an optimized replacement for wasMoved(dest); =copy(dest, x).
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. =dup with nodestroy created independent copy.

claims.claim_text: lent T provides immutable borrow instead of copying. It is a hidden pointer like var T but immutable.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. lent int returned from container accessor compiled and returned correct value.

claims.claim_text: sink parameters are affine, not linear: the callee may consume the value once, or not at all.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Both consuming and non-consuming sink params compiled and destroyed correctly.

claims.claim_text: If compiler cannot prove last use for sink argument, it inserts a copy (=dup or =copy) before passing.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. copyCount=1 confirmed compiler called =copy when source used after sink call.

claims.claim_text: Hooks must be declared before procs using the type, or compiler errors. Hard requirement for generics.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. 'cannot bind another =destroy' error for generic types used before hooks.

claims.claim_text: importc procs are opaque and do not trigger implicit hook generation, so they may safely appear before hooks.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTABLE without C library interop. Accepted from official compiler behavior.

claims.claim_text: Only templates may safely appear between the type definition and the hooks.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Templates do not trigger hook generation.

claims.claim_text: move(x) forces move semantics. It calls =wasMoved on the source.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. move(a) transferred ownership. Source was nil afterward.

claims.claim_text: ensureMove(x) is a compile-time annotation that implies no runtime operation. Works for rvalues and sink params.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. Works for rvalues. Fails compilation when implicit copy needed.

claims.claim_text: swap(a, b) is a builtin that swaps every field via copyMem. It is NOT implemented as let tmp = move(b); b = move(a); a = move(tmp).
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTED. Accepted from official docs.

claims.claim_text: =wasMoved(x) followed by =destroy(x) cancel each other out. The compiler eliminates the destroy call entirely.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. Compiler completely eliminates =destroy after =wasMoved (0 destroy calls vs 1 in normal case).

claims.claim_text: =destroy is implicitly annotated .raises: []. A destructor should not raise exceptions.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. =destroy compiles without explicit .raises: [].

claims.claim_text: {.cursor.} pragma breaks up ref-counting cycles. A cursor field is a non-owning raw pointer.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTED. Accepted from official docs.

claims.claim_text: Cursor inference (copy elision): in dest = src, if neither is mutated afterward, the copy is elided.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTED. Accepted from official docs.

claims.claim_text: {.nodestroy.} pragma inhibits all hook injections for a proc. You must call =destroy manually.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. {.nodestroy.} proc had 0 destroy calls vs 1+ in normal proc.

claims.claim_text: String literals use copy-on-write. prepareMutation needed before addr operations.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTED. Accepted from official docs.

claims.claim_text: Object and tuple fields are treated as separate entities for last-use analysis in sink parameter checking.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. Consuming tup[0] via sink leaves tup[1] accessible.

claims.claim_text: Sink parameter inference can be enabled with --sinkInference:on.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTED. Accepted from official docs.

claims.claim_text: =trace has a mutual use problem with =destroy: whichever is defined first auto-generates a version of the other. Forward-declare the second to prevent conflicts.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. Forward declarations of =destroy and =trace prevent mutual-use conflict.

claims.claim_text: Compiler-generated hooks for objects can be overridden by providing custom implementations.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED. All custom hook tests demonstrate overriding compiler-generated hooks.

claims.claim_text: Hooks are statically bound and potentially inlined, not dynamically dispatched.
claims.evaluation_notes: NOT TESTABLE without inspecting generated C code. Accepted from official docs.

claims.claim_text: When implementing constructors that allocate backing storage, guard against zero-length inputs. alloc(0) may return nil or an invalid pointer; accessing data[0] on zero-length allocation causes index-out-of-bounds.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. Derived from benchmark: 3/6 trials failed on initString("") due to missing zero-length guard.

claims.claim_text: Prefer =destroy(x: T) over =destroy(x: var T). Both compile, but the non-var form prevents accidental field mutation inside the destructor body.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. Both forms compile. var form allows x.data = nil inside destroy; non-var form does not.

claims.claim_text: In =copy for deep-owning containers, after destroy + wasMoved, check that source data is non-nil before allocating and copying.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED in cycle 2. =copy with nil guard handles empty-to-full and full-to-empty transitions.

claims.claim_text: With the inverted counter convention (`counter == 0` unique, `counter > 0` shared), refcounted `=copy` can omit a pointer self-assignment guard if destroy-then-share keeps aliasing cases balanced.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED against the local cowstrings-style pattern. Alias assignment on the same payload stays balanced without a pointer guard.

claims.claim_text: With the inverted counter convention, refcounted `=dup` can omit `{.nodestroy.}` when the refcount increment keeps the return-path destroy balanced.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED against the local cowstrings-style pattern. `=dup` without `{.nodestroy.}` preserves the shared payload and the counter stays balanced.

claims.claim_text: The inverted counter convention is valid and preferred for this repo's CoW-style strings: `counter == 0` means unique ownership, `counter > 0` means shared ownership.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED against the local cowstrings-style pattern. Fresh payloads start at `0` and shared payloads move above `0`.

claims.claim_text: When a refcounted payload may be shared across threads, use `when compileOption("threads")` to switch between `allocShared`/`deallocShared` and `alloc`/`dealloc` at compile time.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED on Nim 2.3.1 by compiling and running the same allocation helper once under the default ORC configuration (`threads:on` by default) and once with explicit `--threads:off`.

claims.claim_text: In the inverted counter convention, `=destroy` should check counter value, not just nil: if `counter == 0`, free directly; if `counter > 0`, decrement.
claims.evaluation_notes: VERIFIED against the local cowstrings-style pattern. Shared payloads decrement first and unique payloads free directly.

uncovered_topics: The preferred inverted-counter CoW path still needs an explicit dataset claim and test for detach-on-mutation behavior, not just share/copy/destroy balance.
needs_stronger_tests: Manual-memory claims such as C03, C33, and C35-C40 are functionally covered, but allocator-level leak freedom should still be spot-checked with an external tool after the normal test passes. In this repo, both ASan and Valgrind were confirmed on test_c35_copy_nil_guard.nim.

corrections_vs_original_skill.original_claim: Hooks must be declared before procs using the type or the compiler will error.
corrections_vs_original_skill.correction: This is true for generic types and complex usage patterns. Simple procs in the same module may not trigger the error on Nim 2.3.1.

corrections_vs_original_skill.original_claim: ensureMove(x) checks that the compiler can prove a move is legal.
corrections_vs_original_skill.correction: More precisely: ensureMove is an annotation that implies no runtime operation. It works for rvalues and sink parameters. It fails for normal params.

corrections_vs_original_skill.original_claim: {.error: "custom error".} on =copy emits the custom error.
corrections_vs_original_skill.correction: Official docs explicitly state: custom error messages will not be emitted by the compiler. Only bare {.error.} prevents the copy.
